generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor converting electrical current (I_M) into rotational torque (τ_M) for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot"
]

instance Gearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Comprises a gearbox followed by a cycloidal gearbox reducing torque (τ_G → τ_out) while adjusting rotational speed for precise motion control"
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Specialized gearbox component reducing torque and adjusting rotational speed for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Directly mounted on the PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor to measure rotational position and velocity"
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Located behind the Gearbox to detect output angle and velocity of the gearbox system"
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures input current (I_M) via Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller to derive motor torque using manufacturer-specific constants"
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance MotorVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance GearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance GearboxVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance MotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentData
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance FrictionCharacterizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box and black-box friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) implemented in MATLAB/Simulink for real-time identification of static, kinetic, and viscous friction components"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkEnabler
]

instance GearDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamics model estimating gearwheel and system output torques (τ_G, τ_out) from input parameters (angle, velocity, acceleration)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkEnabler
]

instance IdealSystemBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink simulation of ideal system behavior without friction effects"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkEnabler
]

instance FrictionEffectsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink simulation incorporating friction effects (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) to compute simulated torque/velocity traces"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkEnabler
]

instance RobotAssetModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) and Internal Block Diagram (IBD) describing the first axis structure (motor, gearbox, cycloidal gearbox) and interconnections"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance RobotOperationalConstraintsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML model describing physical properties (gear ratios, encoder resolutions) and operational constraints (-90° to 90° range, 75°/s max speed)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance MotorTorqueCalculationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Torque derivation model computing motor torque (τ_M) from input current (I_M) using manufacturer-specific constants"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkEnabler
]

instance DataTraceCollection : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated data traces including I_M, r_M, r_G, r_out with metadata tracing their origin (sensor channels or simulation models)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionData, MotorVelocityData, GearboxPositionData, GearboxVelocityData, MotorTorqueData
]

instance FrictionModelOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated friction-affected torque/velocity traces (τr_M, τr_G, τr_out) from friction models"
    DTDFVocab:fromData FrictionEffectsModel
]

instance LiveSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time sensor data streamed via MQTT protocol from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D controller (I_M, angles, velocities, torque)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData, MotorPositionData, MotorVelocityData, GearboxPositionData, GearboxVelocityData
]

instance SimulationOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated system outputs (τ_M, τ_G, τ_out) derived from dynamics models"
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearDynamicsModel, MotorTorqueCalculationModel
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTProtocolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time sensor data streaming protocol using MQTT for transmitting data (I_M, angles, velocities, torque) from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller"
    DTDFVocab:enables LiveSensorDataTransmissionService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShellEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital Shadow Framework using Asset Administration Shell (AAS) and Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM) to structure data traces, models, and metadata"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTraceManagementService
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB/Simulink for simulation modeling of friction effects (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) and dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService, ControlOptimizationService
]

instance CustomDashboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom visualization dashboard integrating live data, simulations, and deviations (torque/velocity traces) for real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance SysMLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML for structural/behavioral modeling of the first axis (BDD/IBD) describing components and interconnections"
    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralModelingService
]

// C6: Services
instance LiveSensorDataTransmissionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time data streaming service using MQTT protocol to transmit sensor data (I_M, angles, velocities, torque) from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D controller"
    DTDFVocab:provides LiveSensorData
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time detection of static, kinetic, and viscous friction components using white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) models"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCharacterizationInsight
]

instance ControlOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic updates to friction models improving robot motion accuracy by reducing positional deviations (±0.1 mm)"
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionAccuracyImprovementAction
]

instance DiagnosticDecisionMakingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Early detection of wear, tool changes, or environmental impacts (temperature) to adjust control parameters in real-time"
    DTDFVocab:provides WearDetectionAction
]

instance DataTraceManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Structuring and managing data traces with provenance metadata using AAS and DSRM"
    DTDFVocab:provides TraceabilityInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Unified visualization dashboard showing live sensor data, simulated friction models, and deviations between measured/simulated outputs"
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviationAnalysisInsight
]

instance StructuralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "SysML modeling of structural components (motor, gearbox, cycloidal gearbox) and their interconnections"
    DTDFVocab:provides StructuralAnalysisInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionCharacterizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time identification of static, kinetic, and viscous friction components using white-box and black-box models"
]

instance MotionAccuracyImprovementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic updates to friction models reducing positional deviations (±0.1 mm) for improved motion accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance WearDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Early detection of wear, tool changes, or environmental impacts to adjust control parameters in real-time"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance TraceabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Ensuring data provenance and model consistency through structured data traces using DSRM"
]

instance DeviationAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comparison between measured and simulated torque/velocity traces to identify system deviations"
]

instance StructuralAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural analysis of the first axis components (motor, gearbox, cycloidal gearbox) using SysML models"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Not explicitly detailed in the documents; however, the DT likely enables **real-time friction model updates** via the Asset Administration Shell (AAS) framework to dynamically adjust control algorithms for improved motion accuracy. The DT integrates with the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller to refine friction models using live data, ensuring closed-loop optimization."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates on a **real-time, event-driven time scale** with:
  - **Data Sampling Frequency**: Sensor data (I_M, angles, velocities) is streamed via MQTT at **high-frequency intervals** (not explicitly quantified but assumed to be in the **kHz range** for dynamic friction modeling).
  - **Model Update Frequency**: Friction models are continuously refined during operation, with **closed-loop synchronization** between the physical system and DT to adjust control parameters in real-time."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is structured as:
  - A **centralized digital shadow** for the **first axis** of the MABI MAX 100 robot, comprising:
    - **Physical Components**: Motor, gearbox, cycloidal gearbox, encoders, and CNC controller.
    - **Digital Components**: SysML models (BDD/IBD), MathWorks simulation models, MQTT data streams, and a visualization dashboard.
  - The DT is **purpose-driven**, focusing on friction analysis and motion accuracy improvement. Future extensions may include **multi-axis digital shadows** or integration with external systems (e.g., machining tooling) via horizontal integration."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT covers the **operational lifecycle stage** of the robot, focusing on:
  - **As-Operated Representation**: Real-time monitoring and analysis of friction behavior during actual machining tasks.
  - **Dynamic Adaptation**: Continuous refinement of friction models to account for wear, environmental changes (e.g., temperature), or tool interactions."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Verification**: SysML models (BDD/IBD) and simulation models (MATLAB/Simulink) are validated against physical measurements (e.g., encoder data).
  - **Uncertainty Handling**: The difference between measured and simulated outputs (e.g., torque/velocity traces) is used to identify friction losses and model inaccuracies.
  - **Model Calibration**: Manufacturer-specific parameters (e.g., motor constants) are used to derive accurate torque estimates.
  - **Continuous Learning**: Friction models are dynamically updated during operation to adapt to wear, environmental changes, or tool interactions, ensuring high validity over time."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT technical connection relies on:
  - **Network Protocol**: MQTT for real-time data streaming between the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller and the DT.
  - **Data Linkage**: Data traces are linked to their sources (sensors or simulations) via metadata, enabling semantic support and traceability.
  - **Integration Framework**: Asset Administration Shell (AAS) to manage digital shadow components and their interactions with the physical system."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Not explicitly detailed in the documents; however, the DT is likely deployed on a **centralized server** or cloud infrastructure to host:
  - SysML models (BDD/IBD).
  - MathWorks MATLAB/Simulink simulations.
  - MQTT data streams and visualization dashboards.
  - The DT may leverage **real-time computing** capabilities to process high-frequency sensor data."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Not explicitly detailed in the documents; however, potential horizontal integration may include:
  - **External Systems**: Linking with machining tooling data (e.g., tool wear, cutting parameters) to refine friction models.
  - **Other Digital Twins**: Extending the DT to other robot axes or sub-systems (e.g., gearboxes, motors) via modular digital shadow instantiations."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Not explicitly detailed in the documents; however, the data ownership is likely governed by:
  - **Physical System Owner**: The robot manufacturer (MABI Robotic AG) owns the physical data (sensor measurements, operational logs).
  - **DT Service Provider**: The research team (RWTH Aachen University) owns the derived data (simulated outputs, friction models) and may share insights with the robot owner for control improvements."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to:
  - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: UML-based reference model (Fig. 1) for structuring data traces, models, and metadata.
  - **SysML**: Standard modeling language for structural/behavioral descriptions of the robot’s first axis.
  - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Standard for managing digital shadow components and their interactions with the physical system."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
  - **Data Encryption**: MQTT protocol may include TLS for secure data transmission between the CNC controller and DT.
  - **Safety Cell**: The robot’s safety cell restricts the working range to [-90°, 90°], preventing accidental collisions.
  - **Remote Operation**: The DT enables remote monitoring and control of the robot’s first axis, but **human-in-the-loop** oversight is recommended to mitigate risks during machining tasks."
]
